introduction: Random blood glucose of >8.0 mmol/L. Preterm infants particularly when treated with 10% dextrose infusion or total parenteral nutrition are prone to it. Hypothermia, sepsis, post-operative cases, neonatal diabetes (transient or persistent) are also associated.

features: Transient diabetes is suspected among infants with wasting or intra-uterine growth restriction who develop hyperglycaemia whilst on enteral feeds.

non_drug: Reduce the concentration of dextrose in infusion.
Calculate the GIR glucose rate; if > 12 mg/kg/min: Reduce GIR and rate of flow
drug: Insulin 0.05 - 0.1 IU/kg/hour should be commenced if RBG remains above 10 mmol/L with glycosuria despite reduction of glucose concentration in infusion.
Starting dose of insulin infusion 0.05-0.1 U/kg/hr
supportive_measures: Blood glucose > 180 mg/dL (>10 mmol/L) with glucometer then recheck
Confirm with a whole blood measurement in the laboratory
Assess infant for possible underlying cause and treat, including sepsis, excess glucose intake, other illnesses, exogenous corticosteroids or inotropes
Initially glucose should be monitored every 30 min for first hour, hourly thereafter, once glucose stabilized measure 4hourly
